title: Inventory e-waste flow, processing, exposure, and worker-data sources
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates usable, limited, and rejected sources for identifying informal e-waste processing and assessing exposure pathways.
inputs: ILO E-waste and employment around the world
site-level environmental and human sampling studies
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks every source for direct URL, source date, access date, processing activity, geographic grain, method, and explicit non-use limitation.
done_condition: At least six candidate sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering processing activity, exposure measurement, geographic grain, informal-sector coverage, and intervention relevance, and a second reviewer can replicate the classification from the recorded method.

title: Review evidence for identifying active informal e-waste processing clusters
outcome: A reproducible candidate-cluster specification that distinguishes processing activity from general waste, formal facilities, and residential proximity.
expected_artifact: validation.md update with cluster inclusion, exclusion, uncertainty, and missingness rules.
validation_method: Replicator applies the specification to a declared sample of sites and reports false positives, false negatives, and unresolved classifications.
done_condition: A reviewer can reproduce the candidate-cluster classification on a declared sample and identify every site excluded or marked uncertain.

title: Separate e-waste proxy signals from measured exposure pathways
outcome: An exposure-pathway matrix linking processing methods to air, dust, soil, water, food, take-home, and occupational measurements with explicit evidence boundaries.
expected_artifact: evidence.md and validation.md updates with pathway decision rules.
validation_method: Domain reviewer checks that each pathway has a source, measurement boundary, confounding risks, and a non-use case.
done_condition: Every claimed exposure pathway is labeled proxy, measured, or unknown, no proxy is presented as an individual or neighborhood exposure measurement, and an independent reviewer replicates the pathway classification.

title: Red-team formalization, enforcement, and displacement interpretations
outcome: A failure-mode review of how cluster evidence could trigger unsafe closure, relocation, stigmatization, worker exclusion, or false reassurance.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and playbooks.md updates with publication blocks and mitigations.
validation_method: Red-team reviewer identifies whether each high-risk inference is blocked or gated behind domain, field-reality, and replication review.
done_condition: Every operational recommendation is either blocked, narrowed to a measurement decision, or supported by domain review, red-team review, field-reality review, and replication.

title: Check whether cluster evidence changes a real city decision
outcome: A field-facing review naming the user, decision, timing, livelihood constraints, and non-use cases for a candidate cluster artifact.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with named decision pathway and non-use guidance.
validation_method: Field reviewer documents the decision and misuse risk; a second reviewer checks that the artifact does not imply closure, relocation, or diagnosis.
done_condition: The output has a named user, named decision, explicit non-use cases, and replicated field-reality review before publication.
